name: when-releasing-new-product-orchestrate-product-launch
description:
Use when launching a new product end-to-end from market research through post-launch monitoring.
Orchestrates 15+ specialist agents across 5 phases in a 10-week coordinated workflow including
research, development, marketing, sales preparation, launch execution, and ongoing optimization.
Employs hierarchical coordination with parallel execution for efficiency and comprehensive coverage.
capabilities: Coordinates 15+ specialized agents across 5 workflow phases
Manages sequential and parallel execution patterns
Stores phase deliverables in hierarchical memory structure
Provides pre-built scripts for workflow initialization
Includes comprehensive success metrics and KPIs
Supports adaptive optimization in post-launch phase
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing workflow documentation for product launch orchestration. All 201 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - they represent shell command examples and security terminology in documentation/code blocks, not actual executable code. The skill describes legitimate business workflows and uses standard CLI tooling (npx claude-flow) for orchestration. No network calls, file system access, or security-sensitive operations are performed by this skill itself.
valueStatement: Coordinating product launches across multiple teams is complex and error-prone. This skill orchestrates 15+ specialized AI agents through a proven 10-week workflow covering research, development, marketing, launch, and optimization phases.
userTitle: Orchestrate Complete Product Launches with AI Swarms
limitations: Requires 10-week timeline commitment for full workflow
Needs access to Claude Flow swarm coordination tools
Memory system required for cross-phase data persistence
Complexity may be overkill for simple product launches

useCases.targetUser: Product managers launching new SaaS products
useCases.title: SaaS Product Launch Coordination
useCases.description: Orchestrate development, marketing, and sales teams for a comprehensive SaaS launch with proper sequencing and dependencies.

useCases.targetUser: Startup founders with limited team resources
useCases.title: Resource-Efficient Launch Management
useCases.description: Leverage AI agents to fill expertise gaps across development, marketing, and sales functions for lean teams.

useCases.title: Multi-Stakeholder Launch Alignment
useCases.description: Coordinate complex launches across engineering, marketing, sales, support, and executive stakeholders with clear accountability.

promptTemplates.title: Initialize New Product Launch
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new product launch workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to launch a new product called [PRODUCT_NAME]. Please initialize the complete 10-week product launch workflow with all 15 agents coordinated across the 5 phases from research to post-launch optimization.

promptTemplates.title: Phase 2 Development Kickoff
promptTemplates.prompt: Based on the market research and product strategy from Phase 1, spawn the 6 development agents (backend, frontend, mobile, database, security, QA) to build the product in parallel over 6 weeks.

promptTemplates.title: Launch Day Execution
promptTemplates.prompt: We are ready to launch. Please execute Phase 4 with production validation, deployment, marketing blitz, and real-time monitoring setup for launch week.

promptTemplates.title: Post-Launch Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate the weekly performance report for week [N] post-launch and identify optimization opportunities for conversion rates, support efficiency, and marketing ROI.

bestPractices: Store all phase deliverables in memory using the hierarchical namespace pattern for cross-team visibility
Run security audits in Phase 2 before production deployment to avoid launch delays
Use parallel execution for development and marketing prep to compress timeline while maintaining quality
antiPatterns: Skipping Phase 1 research and jumping straight to development without market validation
Running all phases sequentially instead of overlapping development with marketing preparation
Launching without proper monitoring setup, making it impossible to track launch success metrics

faq.question: Is this workflow compatible with agile development methodologies?
faq.answer: Yes, Phase 2 development uses parallel agile sprints with continuous integration and testing.

faq.question: What are the minimum system requirements for running this workflow?
faq.answer: Requires Claude Flow with swarm coordination, memory system for data persistence, and ability to spawn agents.

faq.question: Can I customize the timeline or skip certain phases?
faq.answer: The workflow is modular. Adjust phase durations or skip phases, but the full timeline is optimized for comprehensive launches.

faq.question: How is sensitive business data protected during the launch process?
faq.answer: All data is stored in your local memory system with namespace isolation. No external APIs or data sharing occurs without configuration.

faq.question: What happens if we encounter critical issues during launch?
faq.answer: Phase 4 includes rollback procedures and production validation ensures go or no-go decisions before full launch.

faq.question: How does this compare to traditional project management tools?
faq.answer: Unlike static Gantt charts, this uses adaptive AI agents that adjust execution based on real-time feedback and dependencies.
